articles.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
articles.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

articles.title: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
articles.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

articles.title: US House votes to end record shutdown over immigration enforcement
articles.summary: The vote provides funding for most Department of Homeland Security agencies, except for two subagencies responsible for immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
articles.summary: At least nine people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

articles.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
articles.summary: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

articles.title: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
articles.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

articles.title: Britney Spears charged in California with driving under influence
articles.summary: The pop star is facing one misdemeanour count of driving under the influence of any alcohol and drug.

articles.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
articles.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

articles.title: Man sentenced to death for murder of toddlers at Ugandan nursery
articles.summary: Christopher Okello Onyum had pleaded insanity but this was dismissed by the judge.

articles.title: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital
articles.summary: The blockade comes days after Mali's defence minister was assassinated in a series of nationwide attacks.
